# Gemini Review Pass 1: Fact Checker (RAG-Grounded)

> **You are a fact-checking reviewer for Ukrainian language curriculum.**
> Your ONLY task: compare the module content against authoritative reference sources.
> You do NOT evaluate style, pedagogy, or naturalness — that is a separate reviewer's job.

**IMPORTANT: This content was generated by another AI agent. You are an independent fact-checker.
Compare claims against the provided reference sources. Do NOT evaluate from your own knowledge.**

---

## Module Content

```markdown
## Вступ та культурний контекст (Introduction & Cultural Context)

Welcome to Module 12! You already know how to describe things with adjectives — now you'll learn to talk about **кольори** (colors) and **одяг** (clothing). By the end of this module, you'll describe what someone is wearing, using the right color adjective for each item.

Colors in Ukrainian culture carry deep symbolic meaning. The колір **червоний** (red) — a symbol of life, love, and energy. You'll find it everywhere in Ukrainian folk art — most famously in **червона калина** (red viburnum), the national symbol of Ukraine. The колір **чорний** (black) — a symbol of fertile earth and wisdom. Think of **чорний хліб** (black bread) — a staple of Ukrainian life. And **білий** (white) — a symbol of purity and the divine. Ці три кольори (**червоний**, **чорний**, **білий**) разом (together) — the core palette of Ukrainian folk tradition.

<!-- adapted from: Pavlychko, "Два кольори"; Grade 2 kravcova, folk symbolism -->

The most iconic Ukrainian garment is the **вишиванка** — an embroidered shirt. A **вишиванка** is more than decoration. Traditionally, the embroidery on the **комір** (collar) and **манжети** (cuffs) serves as an **оберіг** (talisman). The **візерунок** (pattern) creates a protective circle around the wearer's neck and wrists — closing off vulnerable points with symbolic designs. Each region of Ukraine has its own color combinations: central Ukraine favors **червоний** and **чорний**, while western regions use more **синій** (blue) and **жовтий** (yellow). Your first Ukrainian phrase about clothing:

- **Це вишиванка.** — This is a vyshyvanka.
- **Ця вишиванка — червона й чорна.** — This vyshyvanka is red and black.

> [!culture]
> **Два кольори — Two Colors**
>
> The beloved poem *«Два кольори»* by Dmytro Pavlychko captures this symbolism perfectly: The lines *«Червоне — то любов, а чорне — то журба»* (Red is love, and black is sorrow). Every Ukrainian knows these lines. The pairing of **червоний** and **чорний** in embroidery tells a story of life's joys and hardships woven together.

---

## Презентація кольорів та граматика узгодження (Colors & Grammar of Agreement)

### Шість основних кольорів — Six Basic Colors

Here are the six colors you need at this level. You already know **синій**, **червоний**, and **зелений** from Module 11. Now let's add **білий**, **чорний**, and **жовтий** to your palette.

| **Колір** (Color) | Masculine (-ий/-ій) | Feminine (-а/-я) | Neuter (-е/-є) | Plural (-і) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| white | **білий** | **біла** | **біле** | **білі** |
| black | **чорний** | **чорна** | **чорне** | **чорні** |
| red | **червоний** | **червона** | **червоне** | **червоні** |
| blue | **синій** | **синя** | **синє** | **сині** |
| green | **зелений** | **зелена** | **зелене** | **зелені** |
| yellow | **жовтий** | **жовта** | **жовте** | **жовті** |

Remember: the adjective ending must match the gender of the noun it describes. You practiced this in Module 11. The same rule applies to colors — the color word changes its ending to agree with the noun.

### Типова помилка — A Common Mistake

English speakers often default to the masculine form for everything. Watch out:

- ❌ ~~червоний сорочка~~ — **сорочка** is feminine!
- ✅ **червона сорочка** — red shirt

Here's how it works with different genders:

- **червоний светр** — red sweater (masculine: -ий)
- **червона сорочка** — red shirt (feminine: -а)
- **червоне плаття** — red dress (neuter: -е)

> [!tip]
> **Quick Gender Check**
>
> Look at the noun ending: **-а/-я** → feminine → color ends in **-а/-я**. Consonant → masculine → color ends in **-ий/-ій**. **-о/-е/-я** (neuter) → color ends in **-е/-є**. You already know these patterns from Module 11!

### Синій — The Odd One Out

Five of the six colors follow the hard-stem pattern (-ий/-а/-е/-і). But **синій** (blue) is a soft-stem adjective: **-ій/-я/-є/-і**. Notice the difference:

- Masculine: **жовтий**, Feminine: **жовта**, Neuter: **жовте**, Plural: **жовті** (hard stem)
- Masculine: **синій**, Feminine: **синя**, Neuter: **синє**, Plural: **сині** (soft stem)

The Ukrainian **прапор** (flag) gives you a perfect way to remember this. The flag has two stripes:

- **синє небо** — blue sky (neuter, soft: -є)
- **жовте поле** — yellow field (neuter, hard: -е)

Both are neuter nouns, but the endings on the colors differ: **синє** vs. **жовте**. That's how you know **синій** is special.

> [!warning]
> **Don't Mix Up Soft and Hard!**
>
> - ❌ ~~синя сорочка~~ ... wait, this IS correct! The feminine form is **синя**, while the masculine is **синій**.
> - ❌ ~~синьа сорочка~~ — no such form exists.
> - ✅ **синя сорочка** — blue shirt. Trust the pattern: **-ій → -я → -є → -і**.

### Незмінювані кольори — Invariable Loan Colors

Some color words borrowed from other languages never change their form. No matter the gender of the noun, these colors stay the same:

- **сукня бордо** — burgundy dress (F)
- **светр бордо** — burgundy sweater (M)
- **плаття бордо** — burgundy dress (N)

The same applies to **беж** (beige) and **хакі** (khaki). These loan colors follow the noun instead of coming before it. Don't add endings to them — that's a common beginner mistake.

---

## Одяг — Clothing Vocabulary

### П'ять основних речей — Five Core Items

Let's learn the essential clothing words. Each one has a gender, and that gender determines how you describe it with a color.

**Сорочка** (shirt, F) — the most culturally loaded clothing word in Ukrainian. A **вишита сорочка** (embroidered shirt) is the **вишиванка** you learned about above. In everyday life, **біла сорочка** (white shirt) is common formal wear.

**Штани** (pants) — always plural in Ukrainian. There is no singular form. You say **нові штани** (new pants), never ~~новий штан~~.

**Сукня** (dress, F) — the more elegant, formal word for a dress. You'll see it in descriptions of special occasions: **червона сукня** (red dress), **нова сукня** (new dress).

**Куртка** (jacket, F) — everyday outerwear. **Тепла куртка** (warm jacket), **чорна куртка** (black jacket).

**Светр** (sweater, M) — the masculine noun in the group. **Синій светр** (blue sweater), **зелений светр** (green sweater).

<!-- adapted from: Kravtsova, Grade 4, p.18 — штани etymology -->

### Описуємо одяг — Describing Clothing

You don't need verbs to describe clothing. Use the adjective + noun pattern you already know:

- **червона сукня** — a red dress
- **синій светр** — a blue sweater
- **білі штани** — white pants
- **чорна куртка** — a black jacket
- **зелена сорочка** — a green shirt

Now with all three genders side by side:

- **чорний светр** — black sweater (M: -ий)
- **чорна куртка** — black jacket (F: -а)
- **чорне плаття** — black dress (N: -е)

> [!practice]
> **Your Turn — Say It Out Loud!**
>
> Look around you. Pick one item of clothing you're wearing. What **колір** (color) is it? Try to say it in Ukrainian. For example: **Це біла сорочка.** or **Це сині штани.**

### Плаття vs. Сукня — Two Words for "Dress"

Ukrainian has two words for "dress." **Сукня** is the more elegant, literary choice. **Плаття** is the everyday, conversational word. Both are correct. The key difference for you as a learner: **сукня** is feminine (-а ending), while **плаття** is neuter (-е/-я ending). This affects which color form you use:

- **червона сукня** — red dress (F: -а)
- **червоне плаття** — red dress (N: -е)

Same item, different grammar!

### Pluralia Tantum — Always Plural

Some clothing words exist only in plural form. You've already met **штани** (pants). Two more:

**Джинси** (jeans) — always plural, just like **штани**:
- **сині джинси** — blue jeans
- **нові джинси** — new jeans

**Окуляри** (glasses) — also always plural:
- **великі окуляри** — big glasses
- **чорні окуляри** — black glasses

With these words, use the special pronoun **одні** (one pair of) instead of "один":

- ❌ ~~один штани~~ or ~~одна штани~~
- ✅ **одні штани** — one pair of pants
- ✅ **одні джинси** — one pair of jeans
- ✅ **одні окуляри** — one pair of glasses

The adjective always takes the plural form (-і): **сині джинси**, **чорні окуляри**, **білі штани**.

> [!note]
> **Pluralia tantum** means "plural only." English has these too — "pants," "jeans," "glasses" are always plural. Ukrainian works the same way here. The demonstrative is **ці** (these): **ці штани**, **ці джинси**, **ці окуляри**.

---

## Практичне застосування — Describing Outfits

### Описуємо людей — Describing What People Wear

You can describe someone's outfit using simple verb-free patterns. You already know **це** (this is) — combine it with a color and a clothing item:

- **Це червона сукня.** — This is a red dress.
- **Ці штани — сині.** — These pants are blue.
- **Мій светр — зелений.** — My sweater is green.
- **Це біла сорочка.** — This is a white shirt.
- **Ця куртка — чорна.** — This jacket is black.

Notice two patterns here. In the first pattern, the adjective comes before the noun: **червона сукня**. In the second pattern, the adjective comes after with a dash: **штани — сині**. Both are natural in Ukrainian.

> **(У крамниці / At a shop)**
>
> — Ця сорочка — біла.
> — А ця?
> — Ця сорочка — синя.
> — Гарна! А ці штани?
> — Ці штани — чорні.

> **(На вулиці / On the street)**
>
> — Це твоя куртка?
> — Так, це моя куртка. Вона зелена.
> — А мій светр — жовтий!

### Вишиванка за регіонами — Regional Styles

Different regions of Ukraine have distinctive **вишиванка** styles. You can describe them with what you've learned:

- **Це традиційна сорочка.** — This is a traditional shirt.
- **Ця вишиванка — червона й чорна.** — This vyshyvanka is red and black. (Central Ukraine: Poltava, Kyiv region)
- **Ця вишиванка — біла й синя.** — Wait — that's wrong! Let's fix it: **Ця вишиванка — біла й синя.** (Western Ukraine: Volyn, Zakarpattia)

The color must agree with **вишиванка** (feminine), not with the pattern itself. You're describing the shirt, so use feminine forms.

> [!challenge]
> **Загадка — Riddle**
>
> Here's a folk-style riddle using only colors and nouns — no verbs needed:
>
> A riddle: *«Червоне, але не сонце. Жовте, але не лимон. Синє, але не небо.»*
>
> (Red, but not the sun. Yellow, but not a lemon. Blue, but not the sky.)
>
> Can you guess? It's the Ukrainian **прапор** and a field of poppies! The point isn't the answer — it's that you can now read and understand color descriptions in Ukrainian. Great job!

---

## Підсумок — Summary

You've made real progress! Here's what you can now do:

You already know six colors — **білий**, **чорний**, **червоний**, **синій**, **зелений**, **жовтий** — and how their endings change for masculine, feminine, neuter, and plural nouns. You know five clothing items — **сорочка**, **штани**, **сукня**, **куртка**, **светр** — plus **плаття**, **джинси**, and **окуляри**. You understand that **синій** is a soft-stem adjective with different endings from the others. You can handle invariable loan colors like **бордо**, **беж**, and **хакі**. And you know that **штани**, **джинси**, and **окуляри** are always plural — paired with **одні**, not ~~один~~.

**Self-check — can you do these?**

1. Say "blue jeans" in Ukrainian with correct agreement.
2. What is the feminine form of **жовтий**?
3. Describe a black jacket: **Це _____ куртка.**
4. Why does **сукня бордо** have no ending on "бордо"?

If you can answer all four, you're ready for the next module on plurals. Don't worry if **синій** still feels tricky — you'll see it again and again. Take your time, and remember: every Ukrainian word you learn opens a window into a rich and beautiful culture.

**Want more practice?** Listen to Ukrainian Lessons Podcast Episode 25 for color pronunciation, or Episode 32 for clothing and shopping vocabulary.
> [!tip]
> **Check Your Stem!**
> Most colors end in **-ий**, but **синій** ends in **-ій**. This small difference means it uses **-я**, **-є**, and **-і** for other forms. Always check if the color is "hard" or "soft" before adding the ending!

> [!example]
> **Моя улюблена вишиванка (My Favorite Vyshyvanka)**
> - Це моя нова вишиванка. (This is my new vyshyvanka.)
> - Вона біла, червона й чорна. (It is white, red, and black.)
> - Цей одяг — мій улюблений! (I love this clothing very much!)

> [!cultural-note]
> **Кольори Прапора (Colors of the Flag)**
> The Ukrainian flag represents a blue sky (**синє небо**) over a yellow field of wheat (**жовте поле**). These two colors became the national symbol in 1848 and were officially restored in 1991 when Ukraine became independent.

```

---

## Plan (Source of Truth for Scope)

```yaml
module: a1-012
level: A1
sequence: 12
slug: colors-and-clothing
version: '2.1'
title: Colors & Clothing
subtitle: Describing What You Wear
focus: vocabulary
pedagogy: PPP
phase: A1.1 [First Contact]
word_target: 1200
objectives:
- Learner can name basic colors in Ukrainian
- Learner can use color adjectives with correct gender agreement
- Learner can describe clothing items
- Learner can describe what someone is wearing
content_outline:
- section: Вступ та культурний контекст (Introduction & Cultural Context)
  words: 250
  points:
  - 'Символізм кольорів в українській традиції: червоний як колір життя та любові, чорний як символ родючої землі та мудрості,
    білий як знак чистоти. Пов''язання з фольклорними образами (червона калина, чорний хліб).'
  - 'Вишиванка як оберіг (talisman): функція вишивки на комірі та манжетах як захисного кола, що охороняє власника. Концепція
    ''закриття'' енергетичних зон через візерунок.'
- section: Презентація кольорів та граматика узгодження (Colors & Grammar of Agreement)
  words: 300
  points:
  - 'Презентація основних кольорів згідно з Державним стандартом (§4.2.1.2): білий, чорний, червоний, синій, зелений, жовтий.'
  - 'Узгодження прикметників з іменниками в роді: детальне відпрацювання закінчень (-ий/-а/-е). Виправлення типової помилки
    ''червоний сорочка'' через контрастні пари: червоний светр (M) vs червона сорочка (F).'
  - Незмінювані кольори-запозичення (бордо, беж, хакі) — пояснення відсутності закінчень незалежно від роду іменника.
- section: Одяг — Clothing Vocabulary
  words: 350
  points:
  - 'Лексика теми ''Одяг'': сорочка, штани, сукня, куртка, светр. Стилістична різниця між синонімами: ''сукня'' (елегантний/офіційний
    варіант) та ''плаття'' (загальна назва).'
  - 'Опис одягу через прикметник + іменник (verb-free): червона сукня, синій светр, білі штани.
    Gender agreement practice with clothing: чорний светр (M), чорна куртка (F), чорне плаття (N).'
  - 'Особливості Pluralia Tantum: штани, джинси, окуляри. Узгодження прикметників у множині (''ці
    штани'', ''нові джинси''); займенник ''одні'' замість ''один''.'
- section: Практичне застосування — Describing Outfits
  words: 300
  points:
  - 'Опис одягу інших людей через verb-free patterns: «Це червона сукня», «Ці штани — сині»,
    «Мій светр — зелений». Practice combining colors with clothing items and gender agreement.'
  - 'Культурний контекст одягу: кольори вишиванки за регіонами, повсякденний стиль українців.
    Описи з використанням прикметників: «Це традиційна сорочка», «Ця вишиванка — червона й чорна».'
vocabulary_hints:
  required:
  - білий (white) — біла сорочка, білий сніг, білий світ; частотне (High Core A1); символ чистоти та божественного світу
  - чорний (black) — чорні штани, чорна кава, чорний хліб; символ родючої землі та мудрості
  - червоний (red) — червона сукня, червона ручка, червона калина; символ життя, любові та енергії
  - синій (blue) — сині джинси, синій светр; частотне вживання для повсякденного одягу
  - зелений (green) — зелена трава, зелений колір
  - сорочка (shirt) — вишита сорочка, біла сорочка, одягати сорочку; частотне (High Core A1); основа традиційного вбрання
  - штани (pants) — нові штани, джинсові штани; plural-only (pluralia tantum); завжди вживається в множині
  - сукня (dress) — вечірня сукня, нова сукня; частотний та стилістично вишуканий синонім до 'плаття'
  - куртка (jacket) — тепла куртка, осіння куртка; частотне (High Core A1)
  recommended:
  - плаття (dress) — розмовний синонім до 'сукня'; літнє плаття
  - жовтий (yellow) — жовте сонце, жовтий лимон
  - светр (sweater) — вовняний светр, синій светр
  - окуляри (glasses) — великі окуляри; plural-only
  - джинси (jeans) — сині джинси; plural-only; відмінюється як 'штани'
activity_hints:
- type: match-up
  focus: Color and clothing names
  items: 30
- type: match-up
  focus: Match colors to items
  items: 20
- type: fill-in
  focus: Describe outfits in pictures
  items: 10
- type: fill-in
  focus: Shopping for clothes
  items: 6
connects_to:
- 'a1-42 (Description: Adverbs)'
- 'a1-54 (Checkpoint: Communication)'
prerequisites:
- a1-11 (Describing Things - Adjectives)

  voice: Patient Supportive Tutor
  role: Vyshyvanka Designer
grammar:
- Color adjectives with agreement
- Clothing vocabulary
- Adjective + noun gender agreement with clothing items
register: розмовний

```

## Activities YAML

```yaml
- type: match-up
  title: "Colors and Clothing Names"
  instruction: "Match each Ukrainian word to its English meaning."
  pairs:
    - left: "білий"
      right: "white"
    - left: "чорний"
      right: "black"
    - left: "червоний"
      right: "red"
    - left: "синій"
      right: "blue"
    - left: "зелений"
      right: "green"
    - left: "жовтий"
      right: "yellow"
    - left: "сорочка"
      right: "shirt"
    - left: "штани"
      right: "pants"
    - left: "куртка"
      right: "jacket"
    - left: "светр"
      right: "sweater"

- type: match-up
  title: "Match Colors to Clothing Items"
  instruction: "Match each color-clothing phrase to its English meaning."
  pairs:
    - left: "червона сукня"
      right: "red dress"
    - left: "синій светр"
      right: "blue sweater"
    - left: "білі штани"
      right: "white pants"
    - left: "чорна куртка"
      right: "black jacket"
    - left: "зелена сорочка"
      right: "green shirt"
    - left: "жовте плаття"
      right: "yellow dress"
    - left: "сині джинси"
      right: "blue jeans"
    - left: "чорні окуляри"
      right: "black glasses"

- type: fill-in
  title: "Describe the Outfit"
  instruction: "Choose the correct color form to complete each sentence."
  items:
    - sentence: "Це _____ сукня."
      answer: "червона"
      options: ["червоний", "червона", "червоне", "червоні"]
      explanation: "Сукня is feminine, so the color takes the feminine ending -а."
    - sentence: "Це _____ светр."
      answer: "синій"
      options: ["синій", "синя", "синє", "сині"]
      explanation: "Светр is masculine, so синій keeps the masculine ending -ій."
    - sentence: "Це _____ плаття."
      answer: "чорне"
      options: ["чорний", "чорна", "чорне", "чорні"]
      explanation: "Плаття is neuter, so the color takes the neuter ending -е."
    - sentence: "Це _____ штани."
      answer: "білі"
      options: ["білий", "біла", "біле", "білі"]
      explanation: "Штани is always plural, so the color takes the plural ending -і."
    - sentence: "Це _____ куртка."
      answer: "зелена"
      options: ["зелений", "зелена", "зелене", "зелені"]
      explanation: "Куртка is feminine, so the color takes the feminine ending -а."
    - sentence: "Це _____ сорочка."
      answer: "жовта"
      options: ["жовтий", "жовта", "жовте", "жовті"]
      explanation: "Сорочка is feminine, so жовтий becomes жовта."
    - sentence: "Це _____ небо."
      answer: "синє"
      options: ["синій", "синя", "синє", "сині"]
      explanation: "Небо is neuter. Синій is soft-stem, so the neuter form is синє (not сине)."
    - sentence: "Це _____ джинси."
      answer: "сині"
      options: ["синій", "синя", "синє", "сині"]
      explanation: "Джинси is always plural, so the color takes the plural form сині."

- type: fill-in
  title: "Shopping for Clothes"
  instruction: "Complete each sentence with the correct word."
  items:
    - sentence: "Ця сорочка — _____."
      answer: "біла"
      options: ["білий", "біла", "біле", "білі"]
      explanation: "Сорочка is feminine, so the color is біла."
    - sentence: "Ці штани — _____."
      answer: "чорні"
      options: ["чорний", "чорна", "чорне", "чорні"]
      explanation: "Штани is plural, so the color takes the plural form чорні."
    - sentence: "Мій светр — _____."
      answer: "зелений"
      options: ["зелений", "зелена", "зелене", "зелені"]
      explanation: "Светр is masculine, so the color keeps the masculine ending -ий."
    - sentence: "Ця куртка — _____."
      answer: "синя"
      options: ["синій", "синя", "синє", "сині"]
      explanation: "Куртка is feminine. Синій is soft-stem, so the feminine form is синя."
    - sentence: "Це плаття — _____."
      answer: "червоне"
      options: ["червоний", "червона", "червоне", "червоні"]
      explanation: "Плаття is neuter, so the color takes the neuter ending -е."
    - sentence: "Ці джинси — _____."
      answer: "сині"
      options: ["синій", "синя", "синє", "сині"]
      explanation: "Джинси is always plural, so the color is сині."

- type: quiz
  title: "Color Agreement Check"
  instruction: "Choose the correct answer."
  items:
    - question: "What is the feminine form of жовтий?"
      options:
        - text: "жовта"
          correct: true
        - text: "жовте"
          correct: false
        - text: "жовтій"
          correct: false
        - text: "жовті"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Feminine adjectives with hard stems end in -а, so жовтий becomes жовта."
    - question: "Which color is a soft-stem adjective with different endings from the others?"
      options:
        - text: "синій"
          correct: true
        - text: "білий"
          correct: false
        - text: "жовтий"
          correct: false
        - text: "чорний"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Синій ends in -ій (soft stem), while the others end in -ий (hard stem)."
    - question: "How do you say 'black jacket' in Ukrainian?"
      options:
        - text: "чорна куртка"
          correct: true
        - text: "чорний куртка"
          correct: false
        - text: "чорне куртка"
          correct: false
        - text: "чорні куртка"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Куртка is feminine, so the adjective takes the feminine ending -а."
    - question: "Which of these clothing words is always plural in Ukrainian?"
      options:
        - text: "штани"
          correct: true
        - text: "сукня"
          correct: false
        - text: "куртка"
          correct: false
        - text: "светр"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Штани (pants) is always plural — there is no singular form."
    - question: "What happens to the color бордо when describing different genders?"
      options:
        - text: "It stays the same — бордо never changes"
          correct: true
        - text: "It becomes борда for feminine"
          correct: false
        - text: "It becomes борде for neuter"
          correct: false
        - text: "It becomes борді for plural"
          correct: false
      explanation: "Бордо is a loan color (invariable) — it never changes its form."
    - question: "How do you say 'one pair of jeans' in Ukrainian?"
      options:
        - text: "одні джинси"
          correct: true
        - text: "один джинси"
          correct: false
        - text: "одна джинси"
          correct: false
        - text: "одне джинси"
          correct: false
      explanation: "For plural-only nouns like джинси, use одні (not один/одна/одне)."

- type: true-false
  title: "True or False?"
  instruction: "Decide whether each statement is true or false."
  items:
    - statement: "The Ukrainian word штани can be used in singular form."
      correct: false
      explanation: "Штани is always plural (pluralia tantum). There is no singular form."
    - statement: "The color синій uses different endings than білий because it is a soft-stem adjective."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Синій is soft-stem (-ій/-я/-є/-і), while білий is hard-stem (-ий/-а/-е/-і)."
    - statement: "To say 'red shirt' in Ukrainian, you say червоний сорочка."
      correct: false
      explanation: "Сорочка is feminine, so the correct form is червона сорочка."
    - statement: "The word бордо changes its ending depending on the gender of the noun."
      correct: false
      explanation: "Бордо is an invariable loan color — it stays the same regardless of gender."
    - statement: "The neuter form of синій is синє."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Soft-stem adjectives use -є for neuter, so синій becomes синє."
    - statement: "Плаття and сукня both mean 'dress' but have different genders."
      correct: true
      explanation: "Сукня is feminine and плаття is neuter, which affects color agreement."

- type: group-sort
  title: "Sort by Gender"
  instruction: "Sort these clothing and color phrases into the correct gender group."
  groups:
    - name: "Masculine (чоловічий)"
      items:
        - "чорний светр"
        - "синій светр"
        - "зелений светр"
    - name: "Feminine (жіночий)"
      items:
        - "біла сорочка"
        - "червона сукня"
        - "чорна куртка"
    - name: "Neuter (середній)"
      items:
        - "жовте плаття"
        - "синє небо"
    - name: "Plural (множина)"
      items:
        - "білі штани"
        - "сині джинси"
        - "чорні окуляри"

- type: unjumble
  title: "Put the Words in Order"
  instruction: "Arrange the words to form a correct Ukrainian sentence."
  items:
    - words: ["сукня", "Це", "червона"]
      answer: "Це червона сукня"
    - words: ["синій", "светр", "Це"]
      answer: "Це синій светр"
    - words: ["куртка", "чорна", "Ця"]
      answer: "Ця куртка чорна"
    - words: ["штани", "Ці", "білі"]
      answer: "Ці штани білі"
    - words: ["зелена", "Це", "сорочка"]
      answer: "Це зелена сорочка"
    - words: ["жовтий", "Мій", "светр"]
      answer: "Мій светр жовтий"
```

---

## Research Notes

# Дослідження: Colors & Clothing

## State Standard Reference
**§4.2.1.2**: "Adjective declension — hard/soft groups, gender agreement" (lines 633–650)
Alignment: This module applies adjective gender agreement (introduced in M11) to color adjectives + clothing nouns. Colors provide a concrete vocabulary set to practice hard-stem (-ий/-а/-е) and soft-stem (-ій/-я/-є) paradigms. The thematic area falls under A1 §3 topic **«купівля»** and **«побут»** (lines 479–556).

---

## Vocabulary Frequency

| Word | Notes | Key collocations |
|------|-------|------------------|
| **червоний** | High Core A1; hard-stem adj | червона сукня, червоний светр, червона калина |
| **синій** | High Core A1; **soft-stem** (-ій/-я/-є) — exceptional among basic colors | сині джинси, синій светр, синє небо |
| **білий** | High Core A1; hard-stem | біла сорочка, білий сніг, білий хліб |
| **чорний** | High Core A1; hard-stem | чорні штани, чорна кава, чорний хліб |
| **зелений** | High Core A1; hard-stem | зелена трава, зелений колір |
| **жовтий** | High Core A1; hard-stem | жовте поле, жовтий лимон |
| **сорочка** | High Core A1; F noun | вишита сорочка, біла сорочка |
| **штани** | Pluralia tantum (always plural); requires «одні», not «один» | нові штани, сині штани |
| **сукня** | F noun; elegant/formal register | нова сукня, червона сукня |
| **плаття** | N noun; colloquial register variant of сукня | літнє плаття |
| **куртка** | F noun; high-frequency seasonal wear | тепла куртка, синя куртка |
| **светр** | M noun; светр (VESUM: 2 forms — standard spelling) | вовняний светр, зелений светр |
| **джинси** | Pluralia tantum; VESUM lemma: джинса (rare) / джинси (standard plural) | сині джинси, нові джинси |
| **окуляри** | Pluralia tantum; VESUM: окуляр (M) → plural only in usage | великі окуляри |
| **беж/хакі** | VESUM: tagged `noninfl` — invariable loanword, no endings | сукня беж, куртка хакі |
| **бордо** | VESUM: noun + noninfl — invariable as color | плаття бордо |
| **вишиванка** | Cultural anchor word; VESUM: F noun | червона вишиванка, традиційна вишиванка |

---

## Cultural Hooks

1. **Вишиванка color symbolism**: Central Ukrainian embroidery pairs **червоний** (life, love, energy) with **чорний** (earth, wisdom, fertility) — never random. The combination is a recognized traditional code. Learners can say: «Це вишиванка. Вона червона й чорна.» — immediately applying two colors and gender agreement in a culturally grounded sentence.

2. **Ukrainian flag — синє та жовте**: The flag provides the cleanest mnemonic for two key colors: **синє небо** (soft-stem adj!) + **жовте поле** (hard-stem adj). Use as a discovery pair: *Why does синє look different from жовте?* This naturally surfaces the hard/soft distinction before the rule is stated.

3. **Загадка for color review**: A simple Ukrainian folk riddle pattern learners can encounter:
   *«Червоне, але не сонце. Жовте, але не пісок. Синє, але не море.»*
   (No answer required — the pattern itself is the exercise.) Verb-free, purely adj + noun. Source: folk oral tradition, no copyright.

---

## Common Learner Errors

1. **Gender agreement default to masculine**: `*червоний сорочка` → `червона сорочка` — English has no gender, so learners apply the masculine form (-ий) universally. Most common error with F nouns (сорочка, куртка, сукня). Textbook models: contrast pairs explicitly (червоний светр M / червона куртка F).

2. **Синій soft-stem confusion**: `*синій`→`*синья` (F) or `*синьо` (N) → correct: **синя** (F), **синє** (N). Синій is the only common basic color with a soft stem. Learners tend to regularize it to *-а/-е. Needs flagging early with the flag example (синє небо, not *синьо небо).

3. **Pluralia tantum — wrong numeral**: `*один штани` or `*одна штани` → correct: **одні штани**. English "a pair of pants" gives no Ukrainian intuition. Same issue with джинси and окуляри. No singular form exists in normal usage (VESUM confirms джинси as standard plural).

4. **Invariable loanwords get gender endings added**: `*бордова сукня` → correct: **сукня бордо** (or: **сукня — бордо**). Learners over-apply the agreement rule they just learned. The лoancolor follows the noun, takes no ending, regardless of gender.

---

## Cross-References

- **Builds on**: `a1-11` (Describing Things — Adjectives) — gender agreement fundamentals already introduced; this module extends it to a new vocabulary domain
- **Prepares for**: `a1-42` (Description: Adverbs) — adverbial modification of colors/descriptions; `a1-54` (Checkpoint: Communication) — integrative use of color/clothing in conversational contexts

---

## Teaching Strategy

- **Discovery exercise**: Show 3 clothing images side-by-side — a red shirt (F), a blue sweater (M), a white dress (N). Label only in Ukrainian: «Це червона сорочка», «Це синій светр», «Це біле плаття». Ask: *What does the ending on the color word change between the three?* Let learners notice -а / -ий / -е before any rule is stated. Then contrast синій vs. зелений in the same exercise to surface the hard/soft distinction.
- **Best context**: Getting dressed, shopping for clothes. The vyschankov context works well because it uses only colors + nouns in nominative — no verbs needed yet.
- **Contrastive pairs for disambiguation**:
  - Hard vs. soft: жовтий/жовта/жовте vs. синій/**синя**/**синє** (flag example)
  - Invariable vs. inflecting: червона куртка vs. куртка бордо
  - Pluralia tantum: ці штани сині (not «цей штан синій»)
- **Textbook model**: The Grade 3/4 textbooks use identification exercises (name + match) before production — align activity sequence: match-up first, fill-in second.

---

## Notes for Content Writing

- M12 is pre-verbal: ALL Ukrainian example sentences must be verb-free (Це + noun/adj, noun phrase labels, Adj + noun). No «Я ношу», no «Вона одягла». All classroom directions in English.
- Синій is structurally exceptional among the 6 basic colors — flag mnemonic is the most reliable hook; highlight in a dedicated pattern box.
- Pluralia tantum section should introduce «одні» as the numeral pairing (одні штани, одні джинси, одні окуляри) even if not drilled deeply — learners need to hear it once to avoid «один штани».
- Invariable loancolors (бордо, беж, хакі) — mention briefly as "bonus advanced colors"; do not drill heavily. One example per word is enough.
- VESUM confirms all 18 vocabulary items. Светр verified (2 morphological entries, standard noun).

---

## Friction Constraints (Past Review Findings)

FRICTION CONSTRAINTS (from past build reviews — DO NOT repeat these errors):
- [GLOBAL] сес-тра is a VALID word division per Правопис 2019 §49. Do NOT mark it as an error. Phonetic syllabification (се-стра) and typographic word division (сес-тра) follow different rules — both are correct in their respective contexts.
- [GLOBAL] Ukrainian textbooks teach a hands-on-EARS test for voicing (закрий долонями вуха), NOT a hand-on-throat test. The hand-on-throat test is a valid phonetics technique but must NOT be attributed to Ukrainian textbooks. Source: Кравцова 2019, Grade 2, p.39.
- [GLOBAL] Do NOT invent Ukrainian words for minimal pairs. "Сір" is NOT a word meaning "grey" — the correct form is "сірий". Use verified minimal pairs only: кит/кіт, бити/біти, лис/ліс.

---

## Your Task: Plan Adherence + Factual Verification

### PART A: Plan Adherence Check (MANDATORY)

The plan YAML above is the source of truth for what the module MUST contain. Check EVERY point systematically:

#### A1. Content Outline Points

For EACH section in `content_outline`, and for EACH `points` entry within that section:
1. Search the module content for evidence that this point is addressed
2. Classify as:
   - **COVERED** — the point is clearly addressed in the content. Quote the relevant sentence(s).
   - **MISSING** — the point does not appear in the content. This is a **MEDIUM** severity issue.
   - **PARTIAL** — some aspect is covered but key details are missing. Note what's missing.

**Output a checklist:**
```
### Plan Point Coverage
- Section "Вступ — Introduction":
  - [x] Point 1: "A1 immersion 10-50%..." — COVERED (line ~5: "...")
  - [ ] Point 2: "Cultural hook: Cyrillic script..." — MISSING
  ...
```

#### A2. Required Vocabulary

For EACH word in `vocabulary_hints.required`:
1. Search the module content (not just the word list section — the entire prose)
2. Check if it appears in the activities YAML (if provided in the content above)
3. Classify as:
   - **PRESENT** — found in prose AND activities
   - **PROSE ONLY** — in prose but not in any activity (LOW)
   - **MISSING** — not in prose at all (**HIGH** severity)

#### A3. Activity Hints

For EACH entry in `activity_hints`:
1. Check if an activity of that `type` exists
2. Check if the `focus` matches
3. Check if the `items` count is met
4. Flag missing activity types as **MEDIUM**

#### A4. Section Word Budgets

For EACH section in `content_outline` with a `words` target:
1. Estimate the word count of the corresponding content section
2. Flag sections >10% under their target as **MEDIUM**

#### A5. Pronunciation Videos (if plan has `pronunciation_videos`)

Check that video links from the plan are embedded in the content. Flag missing embeds as **MEDIUM**.

---

### PART B: Factual Verification

For EVERY grammar explanation, factual claim, cultural assertion, and historical reference in the module content:

1. **Search the reference sources above** for confirming or contradicting evidence
2. **Classify each claim** as one of:
   - **CONFIRMED [Tier 1]** — matches a reference source. Cite the source (chunk ID or source identifier) and quote both the module claim and the reference.
   - **DISCREPANCY [Tier 1]** — contradicts a reference source. HIGH severity. Cite both the module claim and the contradicting reference with chunk ID. Suggest the correct version.
   - **UNVERIFIED** — no reference coverage. Flag explicitly. Do NOT evaluate from your own knowledge. Do NOT mark as correct or incorrect — just "UNVERIFIED".

### Citation Enforcement

Every DISCREPANCY entry MUST include:
- Exact quote from the module content
- Exact quote from the reference source
- **Chunk ID or source identifier** (e.g., "Textbook, Grade 3, chunk_abc123" or "VESUM: lemma X")

Free-text citations with no chunk ID or source identifier carry ZERO weight and will be programmatically discarded.

### Special Checks

1. **Callout boxes** (`[!did-you-know]`, `[!myth-buster]`, `[!culture-note]`, `[!fun-fact]`): These are the #1 source of fabrication. Scrutinize every claim.
2. **Grammar rules**: Verify rule statements against textbook references. Flag overgeneralizations.
3. **Etymologies**: Verify word origins against references. Flag folk etymologies.
4. **Statistics and dates**: Flag unsourced numbers.
5. **VESUM failures**: Words not found in VESUM may be misspellings or Russianisms.

---

## Output Format

You MUST use these exact delimiters:

```
===FACTUAL_REVIEW_START===
## Plan Adherence Summary

**Content outline points:** N total, N covered, N missing, N partial
**Required vocabulary:** N total, N present, N missing
**Activity hints:** N total, N matched, N missing
**Plan Adherence Score:** X/10
(10 = all points covered; deduct 1 per MISSING content point, 2 per MISSING required word)

### Plan Point Coverage

[Checklist from Part A1 — every point with [x] or [ ] and evidence]

### Required Vocabulary Coverage

| Word | In Prose? | In Activities? | Status |
|------|-----------|---------------|--------|
| ... | YES/NO | YES/NO | PRESENT / PROSE ONLY / MISSING |

### Activity Hints Coverage

| Hint Type | Focus | Required Items | Found? | Actual Items |
|-----------|-------|---------------|--------|-------------|
| ... | ... | N | YES/NO | N |

### Missing Plan Points (Fix Targets)

[List each MISSING or PARTIAL point with the exact plan text and which section it belongs to.
These become fix targets in the SECTION_FIX block below.]

---

## Factual Verification Summary

**Total claims checked:** N
**Confirmed [Tier 1]:** N
**Discrepancies [Tier 1]:** N
**Unverified:** N
**Factual Alignment Score:** X/10

## Confirmed Claims

[List confirmed claims with source citations]

## Discrepancies

### Discrepancy 1: [brief title]
- **Module says:** "[exact quote from module]"
- **Reference says:** "[exact quote from reference source]"
- **Source:** [chunk ID / source identifier]
- **Severity:** HIGH
- **Suggested fix:** [what to change]

[... more discrepancies ...]

## Unverified Claims

[List claims with no reference coverage]

## Verdict

**Status:** PASS / FAIL
(FAIL if any HIGH severity discrepancy OR 3+ MISSING plan points found)
===FACTUAL_REVIEW_END===
```

If you find fixable discrepancies, also output FIND/REPLACE pairs:

```
===SECTION_FIX_START===
FILE: /Users/krisztiankoos/projects/learn-ukrainian/curriculum/l2-uk-en/a1/colors-and-clothing.md
FIND:
[exact text from module with the error]
REPLACE:
[corrected text based on reference source]
---
[... more pairs, max 15 ...]
===SECTION_FIX_END===
```

---

## Boundaries

- Do NOT evaluate style, naturalness, or pedagogy — that is Pass 2's job
- Do NOT score dimensions (except Plan Adherence and Factual Alignment) — that is Pass 2's job
- Do NOT evaluate factual claims from your own knowledge — ONLY compare against provided references
- Plan adherence checking uses the plan YAML as ground truth — this is objective, not subjective
- Do NOT fabricate references — if no reference covers a claim, mark it UNVERIFIED
- Maximum 15 FIND/REPLACE pairs (prioritize MISSING plan points over minor factual fixes)
